records.ety: [L. conglomeratus, p. p. of conglomerare to roll together; con- + glomerare to wind into a ball. See Glomerate.]

defs: Gathered into a ball or a mass; collected together; concentrated; as, conglomerate rays of light.
quotes: Beams of light when they are multiplied and conglomerate.
Fluids are separated in the liver and the other conglobate and conglomerate glands.

defs: Closely crowded together; densely clustered; as, conglomerate flowers.
Composed of stones, pebbles, or fragments of rocks, cemented together.
That which is heaped together in a mass or compacted from various sources; a mass formed of fragments; collection; accumulation.
quotes: A conglomerate of marvelous anecdotes, marvelously heaped together.

defs: A rock, composed or rounded fragments of stone cemented together by another mineral substance, either calcareous, siliceous, or argillaceous; pudding stone; -- opposed to agglomerate. See Breccia.
quotes: A conglomerate, therefore, is simply gravel bound together by a cement.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conglomerated; p. pr. & vb. n. Conglomerating.]

defs: To gather into a ball or round body; to collect into a mass.
